codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, implementation, and tests. The adapter correctly wraps Cloudflare Puppeteer into the BrowserLike/BrowserPage contracts, configures viewport and locale on new pages, extracts BrowserPageSnapshot data via page.evaluate(), and delegates lifecycle and page actions as expected. I found no high-confidence correctness, robustness, or regression issues within the reviewed feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission feature spec and cf-worker skill still describe snapshot interactive elements as having 'type' fields, but the runtime contract uses ObservationInteractiveElement.role. That mismatch can mislead future workers even though this implementation correctly follows the existing BrowserPageSnapshot contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission features.json:241 says 'Each interactive element has selector, label, and type fields'; .factory/skills/cf-worker/SKILL.md:94 says snapshot elements should include selectors, labels, and types; apps/browser-executor/src/observationBuilder.ts:5-10 defines ObservationInteractiveElement with role/label/selector, and apps/browser-executor/src/runExecutor.ts:42 uses that type for BrowserPageSnapshot.interactiveElements.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported a justified procedure deviation because unrelated .factory/library files were already dirty, but the available project skills do not document how to handle pre-existing unstaged shared-state edits when a feature is otherwise ready to commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff /Users/alvaro.ybanez/.factory/missions/6ceae915-bba1-41a1-9dec-4ed54e3c81f0/handoffs/2026-03-31T11-33-00-879Z__puppeteer-browser-adapter__f011c7dd-ce24-468a-87d3-2c6690f89a96.json:76-83 shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false for step '3.2 Environment Cleanup' due to pre-existing .factory/library/* changes; searching .factory/skills/**/*.md found no documented cleanup guidance for this case.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, implementation, and tests for puppeteer-browser-adapter and found no blocking or non-blocking code defects within feature scope. I noted two shared-state documentation gaps: the type-vs-role snapshot field mismatch and missing guidance for unrelated pre-existing dirty shared-state files.
